instruction: Deploy WordPress on my OneKE cluster with a dedicated namespace and persistent storage
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy WordPress on OneKE cluster with namespace and persistent storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "10 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - wordpress
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create wordpress namespace"
    params:
      name: wordpress
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for WordPress data"
    params:
      name: wordpress-data
      namespace: wordpress
      size: 10Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy WordPress via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: wordpress
      namespace: wordpress
      release_name: wordpress
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: wordpress-data
        service:
          type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for WordPress pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: wordpress
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=wordpress"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the WordPress access URL"
    params:
      service_name: wordpress
      namespace: wordpress
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify WordPress pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove WordPress Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: wordpress
        namespace: wordpress
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the WordPress PVC"
      params:
        name: wordpress-data
        namespace: wordpress
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the wordpress namespace"
      params:
        name: wordpress

instruction: Remove the Redis deployment from the production namespace on my OneKE cluster
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Remove Redis deployment from production namespace"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "3 minutes"
  risk_level: high
  tags:
    - redis
    - cleanup
    - production
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.app.get_status
    description: "Check current Redis deployment status before removal"
    params:
      release_name: redis
      namespace: production
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.uninstall
    description: "Uninstall Redis Helm release"
    params:
      release_name: redis
      namespace: production
    depends_on:
      - step_01
    on_failure: abort
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster is reachable"
    - type: namespace_exists
      description: "Verify production namespace exists"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Redis pods are terminated"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.deploy
      description: "Re-deploy Redis if removal was unintended"
      params:
        chart: redis
        namespace: production
        release_name: redis
        repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

instruction: Create a namespace called monitoring, deploy Prometheus with a 50Gi volume, and expose its web UI
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up Prometheus monitoring with dedicated namespace, storage, and external access"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "8 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - monitoring
    - prometheus
    - oneke
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create monitoring namespace"
    params:
      name: monitoring
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume for Prometheus data"
    params:
      name: prometheus-data
      namespace: monitoring
      size: 50Gi
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Prometheus via Helm"
    params:
      chart: prometheus
      namespace: monitoring
      release_name: prometheus
      repo_url: https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
      values:
        server:
          persistentVolume:
            existingClaim: prometheus-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Prometheus to become ready"
    params:
      namespace: monitoring
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose Prometheus web UI via NodePort"
    params:
      deployment_name: prometheus-server
      namespace: monitoring
      port: 9090
      service_type: NodePort
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify cluster connectivity"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Prometheus is running"
    - type: service_available
      description: "Verify Prometheus service is accessible"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Prometheus"
      params:
        release_name: prometheus
        namespace: monitoring
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete Prometheus storage"
      params:
        name: prometheus-data
        namespace: monitoring
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove monitoring namespace"
      params:
        name: monitoring

metadata:
  description: "Deploy Jenkins on OneKE cluster with namespace and persistent storage"
  target_cluster: oneke-cluster
  estimated_duration: "16 minutes"
  risk_level: medium
  tags:
    - jenkins
    - oneke
    - helm
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create jenkins namespace"
    params:
      name: jenkins
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create persistent volume claim for Jenkins data"
    params:
      name: jenkins-data
      namespace: jenkins
      size: 15Gi
      storage_class: longhorn
    depends_on:
      - step_01
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Jenkins via Helm chart"
    params:
      chart: jenkins
      namespace: jenkins
      release_name: jenkins
      repo_url: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: jenkins-data
    depends_on:
      - step_02
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for Jenkins pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: jenkins
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=jenkins"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on:
      - step_03
  - id: step_05
    action: oneke.service.get_endpoint
    description: "Get the Jenkins access URL"
    params:
      service_name: jenkins
      namespace: jenkins
    depends_on:
      - step_04
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: cluster_reachable
      description: "Verify kubectl can reach the cluster"
    - type: helm_repo_available
      description: "Verify Helm is installed and working"
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Verify Jenkins pods are running"
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Remove Jenkins Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: jenkins
        namespace: jenkins
    - id: step_91
      action: oneke.storage.delete_pvc
      description: "Delete the Jenkins PVC"
      params:
        name: jenkins-data
        namespace: jenkins
    - id: step_92
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Remove the jenkins namespace"
      params:
        name: jenkins

instruction: Set up a WordPress application with persistent storage and expose it to the internet.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Set up a WordPress application with persistent storage and expose it to the internet."
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create a namespace for the WordPress application."
    params:
      name: "wordpress"
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.storage.create_pvc
    description: "Create a persistent volume claim for WordPress data."
    params:
      name: "wordpress-pvc"
      namespace: "wordpress"
      size: "10Gi"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy WordPress application with the PVC."
    params:
      chart: "bitnami/wordpress"
      namespace: "wordpress"
      release_name: "wordpress"
      values:
        persistence:
          existingClaim: "wordpress-pvc"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose the WordPress application to the internet."
    params:
      namespace: "wordpress"
      deployment_name: "wordpress"
      port: 80
      service_type: "LoadBalancer"
    depends_on: [step_03]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: namespace_available
      description: "Check if the namespace 'wordpress' is available."
  post_checks:
    - type: service_available
      description: "Check if the WordPress service is available."
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete the WordPress namespace upon rollback."
      params:
        name: "wordpress"

version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Create a staging environment, deploy an application, and verify its health."
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create a staging namespace."
    params:
      name: "staging"
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy the application in the staging namespace."
    params:
      chart: "my-app"
      namespace: "staging"
      release_name: "my-app-staging"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.wait_ready
    description: "Wait for the application to be ready."
    params:
      namespace: "staging"
      label_selector: "app=my-app"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: namespace_available
      description: "Check if the namespace 'staging' is available."
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Check if the application pods are running."
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete the staging namespace upon rollback."
      params:
        name: "staging"

instruction: Scale the cluster, deploy a new version of an app, and check its status.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Scale the cluster, deploy a new version of an app, and check its status."
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.cluster.scale_nodes
    description: "Scale up the cluster workers."
    params:
      worker_count: 5
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.upgrade
    description: "Upgrade the application to a new version."
    params:
      chart: "my-app"
      release_name: "my-app-production"
      namespace: "production"
      version: "2.0"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.get_status
    description: "Get the status of the upgraded application."
    params:
      release_name: "my-app-production"
      namespace: "production"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: continue
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: resource_available
      description: "Check if there are enough resources to scale the cluster."
  post_checks:
    - type: pods_running
      description: "Check if the application pods are running after upgrade."
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.app.uninstall
      description: "Uninstall the upgraded application upon rollback."
      params:
        release_name: "my-app-production"
        namespace: "production"

instruction: Create a VM, set up a namespace, and deploy an application within it.

instruction: Create a monitoring stack with Prometheus and Grafana on my OneKE cluster.

instruction: Create a Redis cluster, deploy an application using it, and expose the service.
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Create a Redis cluster, deploy an application using it, and expose the service."
  risk_level: medium
steps:
  - id: step_01
    action: oneke.namespace.create
    description: "Create a namespace for the Redis cluster."
    params:
      name: "redis-cluster"
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_02
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy Redis cluster in the Redis namespace."
    params:
      chart: "bitnami/redis"
      namespace: "redis-cluster"
      release_name: "redis"
    depends_on: [step_01]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_03
    action: oneke.app.deploy
    description: "Deploy the application using the Redis cluster."
    params:
      chart: "my-app"
      namespace: "redis-cluster"
      release_name: "my-app"
    depends_on: [step_02]
    on_failure: rollback
  - id: step_04
    action: oneke.service.expose
    description: "Expose the application service."
    params:
      namespace: "redis-cluster"
      deployment_name: "my-app"
      port: 80
      service_type: "LoadBalancer"
    depends_on: [step_03]
    on_failure: rollback
validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: namespace_available
      description: "Check if the namespace 'redis-cluster' is available."
  post_checks:
    - type: service_available
      description: "Check if the application service is available."
rollback:
  steps:
    - id: step_90
      action: oneke.namespace.delete
      description: "Delete the Redis cluster namespace upon rollback."
      params:
        name: "redis-cluster"
